authoritative_sources.title: The CAHPS Ambulatory Care Improvement Guide: Practical Strategies for Improving Patient Experience
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is the federal patient-safety and care-quality agency. Its ambulatory care improvement guide is public operating guidance for physician practices and ambulatory clinics on appointment access, referral handling, communication, and workflow improvement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Ambulatory access, scheduling, and patient-facing clinic workflow design

authoritative_sources.title: Strategies for Improving Patient Experience with Ambulatory Care: Open Access Scheduling for Routine and Urgent Appointments
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ's open-access scheduling strategy is recognized public guidance for managing appointment access, same-day demand, schedule lag, and backlog reduction in ambulatory settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Scheduling readiness, access management, and backlog control

authoritative_sources.title: Strategies for Improving Patient Experience with Ambulatory Care: Rapid Referral Programs
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ's rapid-referral guidance is a recognized ambulatory workflow reference for referral handoffs, specialist access, and ownership of incomplete or aging referrals.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Referral receipt, handoff ownership, and specialty access coordination

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule CMS-0057-F
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is authoritative for Medicare and Medicaid administrative rules. Its CMS-0057-F fact sheet is official guidance for prior-authorization data exchange, response timing, and denial-reason communication that materially affects ambulatory clinics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Prior-authorization packet handling and authorization-status follow-up

authoritative_sources.title: Resources and Tools To Improve Discharge and Transitions of Care and Reduce Readmissions
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ's transitions-of-care resource hub is a recognized workflow reference for safe outpatient follow-up, discharge handoffs, and transition-task closure in ambulatory settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Post-discharge or consult follow-up and closure readiness

authoritative_sources.title: Transitions of care
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy/Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC is authoritative for federal health IT certification. Its transitions-of-care companion guide is a recognized workflow reference for referral and transition summaries, consultation notes, and interoperable document exchange.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Referral-summary exchange, consult-document receipt, and interoperable handoff support

authoritative_sources.title: FHIR: Advancing Interoperability Standards in the API
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy/Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ONC's interoperability guidance is a recognized workflow and terminology reference for referral note, consultation note, and related clinical-document exchange across ambulatory systems.

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Basics for Providers: Privacy, Security, & Breach Notification Rules
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is authoritative for HIPAA enforcement. This provider guidance is the public baseline for minimum-necessary access, disclosure controls, and breach-sensitive handling in ambulatory operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Privacy-aware access, routing, and disclosure handling

authoritative_sources.title: Compliance Programs for Physicians
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of Inspector General, U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OIG is authoritative for federal healthcare compliance expectations. Its physician-practice compliance guidance is directly relevant to documentation integrity, supported claims posture, audit readiness, and escalation discipline in physician practices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Documentation integrity, audit readiness, and unsupported-closure refusal

workflow_stages.name: referral receipt and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that the referral order, referring source, patient identifiers, insurance context, specialty destination, required attachments, and owner map exist before work proceeds.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: scheduling and appointment access coordination
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether schedule templates, visit types, urgent-versus-routine access rules, provider capacity, and pre-visit prerequisites support advancing the request to booking or reschedule handling.

workflow_stages.name: prior-authorization and packet completeness handling
workflow_stages.description: Compare payer, authorization, attachment, and referral records to decide whether the case may advance, must be reworked, or requires specialized escalation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: consult, transition, and closure support
workflow_stages.description: Track visit completion, consult-note receipt, transition-of-care summaries, outreach loops, and workqueue closure evidence without claiming clinical signoff.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: privacy, conflict, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop in-lane handling and escalate when protected information, clinical interpretation, payer appeal work, or conflicting policy changes the answer.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: referral orders, consult requests, and outside-record attachments
patient registration facts, insurance records, and payer or program identifiers
scheduling templates, provider calendars, visit types, outreach logs, and appointment status records
authorization submissions, payer responses, attachment checklists, and denial or pend messages
pre-visit planning checklists, visit summaries, consult notes, and transition-of-care or discharge summaries
document-management artifacts, task-queue notes, escalation packets, and audit trails
systems_of_record: patient portal or messaging workflow when used for operational outreach

allowed: Classify ambulatory operations work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, rework, transfer, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete operational workflow steps based on documented referral, scheduling, authorization, consult, and closure evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed status summaries, completion records, clinic workflow summaries, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
escalate: Required service-line, payer, jurisdiction, scheduling, authorization, result-routing, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires diagnosis, symptom triage, order entry, result interpretation, medical necessity judgment, privacy-law interpretation, appeal drafting, coding, billing signoff, or legal advice.
A state rule, payer contract, or tenant policy materially changes the workflow or closure criteria.
A privacy, patient-safety, or clinical-boundary concern changes whether the work may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without the required referral, authorization, scheduling, visit, consult, or closure evidence when applicable.
Disclose detailed protected information to an outside party without documented operational ownership and approved routing basis.
Treat ambulatory clinic operations like generic scheduling or CRM administration by ignoring referral, authorization, EHR, or consult artifacts.

domain_failure_modes: a referral is scheduled or closed even though the referral order, attachments, or specialty prerequisites are missing
authorization work is advanced on incomplete packets, stale payer responses, or unresolved pend or denial reasons
abnormal results or consult follow-up tasks are handled like generic office messages and bypass clinician or privacy escalation
EHR, scheduling, referral, and payer systems disagree on current case state and the work is still marked complete
tenant or payer-specific ambulatory workflow differences are guessed instead of retrieved and escalated
industry-specific ambulatory terminology is replaced by generic call-center language that omits referral, authorization, consult, and closure artifacts
operations absorbs clinical, utilization, coding, or appeal authority because the lane boundary is underspecified

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: payer-specific prior-authorization submission content and turnaround rules
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Handled as payer and tenant retrieval dependencies; the pack escalates when payer-specific rules are missing or conflict with the CMS baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: state-specific referral-signature, standing-order, or direct-access requirements
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Handled as jurisdiction-specific dependencies retrieved at runtime; the pack refuses unsupported synthesis when these rules materially change the workflow.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated package because payer, state, and tenant-specific differences are explicitly modeled as retrieval dependencies and escalation triggers.
The lane remains administrative and evidence-backed even when the work sits next to clinical triage, authorization review, or revenue-cycle activity.
